title: Commonwealth Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded MA in Human Rights (Distance Learning) at University of London
summary: Applications are now open for a fully funded MA in Human Rights (Distance Learning) offered by the University of London in partnership with the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission . This opportunity is designed for individuals from eligible Commonwealth countries who are unable to study in the UK due to financial or other constraints. The scholarship supports postgraduate study while allowing students to learn remotely from anywhere in the world. A total of 5 full scholarships are available for the September 2026 intake . About the MA in Human Rights The MA in Human Rights is delivered by the School of Advanced Study at the University of London and provides a comprehensive understanding of human rights law, policy, and practice. The distance learning format makes the program accessible to professionals and students who want to advance their education without relocating. It is particularly suited for individuals already working in advocacy, law, development, or social justice. The program covers critical issues such as: Human rights law and policy Protection of marginalized groups Environmental justice and global inequalities Contemporary human rights challenges Scholarship Benefits The Commonwealth Scholarship provides full financial support for selected candidates. Benefits include: Full tuition coverage for the MA program Opportunity to study at a globally recognized institution Flexible distance learning format Access to academic resources and expert faculty This scholarship enables students to gain a high-quality postgraduate education without the financial burden of international study. Who Should Apply The scholarship is targeted at individuals who are passionate about promoting human rights, particularly for marginalized and underserved communities. Ideal candidates include those with a demonstrated track record in: Human rights advocacy Social justice initiatives Legal or policy work Community development Applicants must show a strong commitment to using their education to create positive change in their communities. Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the scholarship, applicants must: Be from eligible low and middle-income Commonwealth countries Be unable to study in the UK due to financial or other constraints Have applied for and received an offer to the MA in Human Rights program Demonstrate a commitment to promoting human rights Applicants are encouraged to review the full list of eligible countries before applying. Important Deadlines Recommended deadline to apply for the MA programme: March 16, 2026 Scholarship application deadline: March 31, 2026 Applicants are advised to complete their MA application early to ensure eligibility for the scholarship. How to Apply Step 1: Apply for admission to the MA in Human Rights programme https://lnkd.in/eGACGFjy Step 2: Review eligible countries https://lnkd.in/eNWJhXYu Step 3: Apply for the scholarship through the official portal https://lnkd.in/esKR3eWq For more Scholarship Opportunities, Visit OFY Here. The post Commonwealth Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded MA in Human Rights (Distance Learning) at University of London appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Green Financing Opportunities for Women Entrepreneurs in Africa: GIZ Uganda Initiative
summary: Kampala, Uganda – In a landmark move to empower women-led businesses and accelerate Africa’s transition to a sustainable economy, GIZ Uganda , in partnership with the IYBA WE4A Project and Melanin Kapital , has launched a dedicated green financing initiative targeting women entrepreneurs across multiple African countries. The program is specifically designed to support enterprises that contribute to environmental sustainability and climate resilience, providing targeted financial solutions to help these businesses scale effectively. Expanding Access to Green Finance for Women Entrepreneurs Women entrepreneurs in Africa continue to face systemic barriers to accessing financing, particularly in sectors related to climate and environmental sustainability. By addressing these challenges, the new program aims to fill a critical funding gap, enabling women-led businesses to thrive while contributing to environmental goals. The initiative is open to women-led businesses operating in eight African countries : Cameroon, Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Senegal, Tanzania, Togo, and Uganda. Through this multi-country approach, the program seeks to maximize the reach of green financing solutions and create a supportive ecosystem for women entrepreneurs across the continent. Eligible businesses are expected to operate within key green industries , including: Clean technology – such as solar and energy-efficient systems. Renewable energy solutions – including solar, wind, and bioenergy applications. Sustainable agriculture – innovative farming methods that reduce environmental impact. Circular economy solutions – businesses that promote recycling, waste reduction, and sustainable resource use. Eco-friendly manufacturing – products designed with minimal environmental footprint. These sectors are increasingly recognized as critical to Africa’s sustainable development, yet they often struggle to attract sufficient investment due to perceived risks and limited access to capital. By focusing on these industries, the program aims not only to support women entrepreneurs but also to contribute to broader environmental and climate objectives , including reduced emissions, improved resource efficiency, and enhanced climate adaptation strategies. Tailored Financial Products for Every Stage of Business Growth A key component of the initiative is its focus on flexible financing structures designed to meet the needs of businesses at different stages of development. The program offers two distinct financial products: Green Asset Financing Designed for smaller-scale investments. Loan amounts range from $1,000 to $10,000 . Supports the acquisition of green technologies such as solar systems, energy-efficient equipment, and e-mobility solutions. Repayment periods span 9 to 12 months with weekly repayment schedules , offering manageable terms for emerging enterprises. Working Capital Loan Tailored for more established businesses managing larger operations or orders. Loan amounts range from $50,000 to $85,000 . Designed to support inventory purchases, operational expansion, and scaling business activities. Repayment terms are 6 to 9 months , with monthly installments aligned with business cash flow cycles. Through these offerings, Melanin Kapital ensures that women entrepreneurs—whether just starting or scaling—can access the capital they need without facing restrictive or one-size-fits-all financing conditions. Eligibility and Application Process To qualify for financing, applicants must meet the following criteria: Be a registered business with at least six months of operational history . Demonstrate a minimum annual revenue : $5,000 for Green Asset Financing and $10,000 for Working Capital Loans. Show tax compliance and provide verifiable documentation, such as invoices, purchase orders, or financial statements. The application process operates on a rolling basis , enabling entrepreneurs to apply when their business is ready, rather than adhering to fixed deadlines. This flexible approach ensures that women-led enterprises can access funding at the most strategic point in their growth journey. APPLY HERE Driving Africa’s Green Economy Through Women-Led Enterprises The GIZ Uganda green financing program represents a strategic effort to empower women entrepreneurs while advancing Africa’s green economy . By combining financial support with a focus on environmental impact , the initiative positions itself as a catalyst for inclusive and sustainable business growth across the continent. Entrepreneurs participating in this program will not only benefit from access to tailored funding but will also join a growing network of innovative and climate-focused enterprises , contributing to a more sustainable and resilient Africa. As the demand for green solutions continues to rise, programs like this play a crucial role in bridging the financing gap, promoting gender equality in business, and accelerating Africa’s journey toward a low-carbon, environmentally sustainable future . title: Carnegie India Young Ambassadors Program 2026–2027: Paid Policy Opportunity in Technology & Society
summary: Applications are now open for the Young Ambassadors Program (CIYAP) under the Technology Society Program at Carnegie India . This one-year, in-person program based in New Delhi , India offers a unique opportunity for recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in research-driven policymaking within a global think tank environment. Running from May 2026 to May 2027 , the program is designed to equip future leaders with critical policy skills, research experience, and exposure to global policy conversations. About the Carnegie India Young Ambassadors Program The Young Ambassadors Program is tailored for early-career individuals who want to understand how policy is shaped through research and analysis. Participants will work closely with experts and scholars at Carnegie India, contributing to policy discussions on some of the most pressing global and regional issues. The program offers an immersive experience in policymaking, allowing participants to engage with multidisciplinary research across areas such as technology, governance, and international affairs. Program Experience and Learning Opportunities The selected ambassador will gain practical exposure by working on real-world policy projects and contributing to the work of a globally recognized think tank. Key experiences include: Observing and engaging in research-driven policymaking Collaborating with experts across global Carnegie offices, including Washington D.C., Brussels, Beirut, Berlin, California, and Singapore Participating in policy discussions and contributing to research outputs Supporting major events such as the Global Technology Summit and other high-level dialogues Building professional skills in leadership, analytical thinking, and report writing Participants will also be mentored by leading scholars and gain valuable insights into how policy decisions are developed and implemented. Areas of Research The program focuses on a wide range of technology and policy-related topics. Applicants may work on areas such as: Artificial intelligence and emerging technologies Digital public infrastructure Cybersecurity and data governance Space policy and defense innovation Semiconductors and critical minerals Nuclear energy and subsea cables This broad scope allows participants to explore their research interests while contributing to impactful policy work. Roles and Responsibilities The selected candidate will support Carnegie India’s research and program activities through a variety of tasks. Responsibilities may include: Conducting research and analysis on technology policy topics Supporting scholars on ongoing research projects Assisting with writing, editing, and report preparation Contributing to event organization and coordination Providing administrative and programmatic support The role is designed to provide a well-rounded experience across research, communication, and program management. Benefits of the Program Participants will receive both professional and financial support during the program. Benefits include: Monthly stipend One-time relocation allowance Mentorship from leading policy experts Exposure to global policy networks Hands-on experience in a leading think tank This opportunity provides a strong foundation for careers in public policy, research, and international affairs. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Recent graduate in any discipline Interest in areas such as technology policy, artificial intelligence, law, economics, or related fields Ability to work full-time in person in New Delhi Additional preference is given to applicants from beyond metropolitan cities, as part of the program’s effort to expand access to policy opportunities. Application Requirements Interested candidates must submit: CV Cover letter One writing sample Applications will be evaluated based on merit, without discrimination based on background or identity. Program Duration May 2026 – May 2027 How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare the required documents and submit their application through the official Carnegie India channels. For more Fellowship oppotunities from OFY, Click here. title: Apply Now: UNITAR & Google Free Digital Skills Training 2026 in Selangor (AI and Data Centres)
summary: On 13 March 2025 in Geneva, Switzerland, the United Nations Institute for Training and Research officially announced a new call for applications for its flagship skills development initiative titled “Pathway to Prosperity: Upskilling Selangor’s Professionals for a Digital Future (Cohort 2).” This fully funded training programme is designed to empower professionals with in-demand digital skills, particularly in data centre management and artificial intelligence (AI) —two of the fastest-growing sectors in today’s global economy. As countries accelerate their digital transformation strategies, regions like Selangor are emerging as critical hubs for innovation, infrastructure development, and tech-driven employment. This programme directly responds to that growth by equipping local talent with practical, job-ready skills aligned with industry needs. Overview of the Programme The Pathway to Prosperity programme is a free, structured training course aimed at building technical capacity among early- and mid-career professionals. Delivered through a blend of virtual and in-person learning, the initiative focuses on bridging the skills gap in Malaysia’s digital economy. Participants will gain foundational and practical knowledge in: Data centre operations and management Artificial intelligence concepts and applications Digital transformation trends and workforce readiness By combining theoretical learning with hands-on exposure, the programme ensures that learners are not only knowledgeable but also capable of applying their skills in real-world scenarios. Why This Programme Matters Selangor plays a pivotal role in Malaysia’s digital growth, hosting a significant share of the country’s data infrastructure and technology investments. As demand for skilled professionals continues to rise, initiatives like this are essential to: Strengthen the local workforce Enhance employability in high-growth sectors Support career transitions into tech-driven roles Align workforce capabilities with national digital priorities This programme is particularly valuable for individuals looking to future-proof their careers in an increasingly digital job market. Course Structure and Learning Format The training will run from 6 April to 28 June 2026 , offering a flexible yet comprehensive learning experience. The structure has been carefully designed to accommodate working professionals while maintaining high-quality instruction. Key Components of the Course 1. Live Online Webinars Held on weekends Duration: Approximately 1.5 hours per week Focused on core concepts, industry insights, and interactive discussions 2. In-Person Workshops Total of three days of face-to-face sessions Scheduled for 13, 27, and 28 June 2026 Location: Selangor area Designed for practical application, networking, and hands-on training 3. Certification Participants who successfully complete the programme will receive a joint certification from UNITAR and Google This certification adds significant value to professional credentials and enhances employability Who Should Apply One of the most appealing aspects of this programme is its accessibility. It is a fundamental-level course , meaning applicants do not need an advanced technical background to participate. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be an early- or mid-career professional Be currently based in Selangor, Malaysia Have a strong interest in data centres or artificial intelligence Be motivated to upskill or transition into the digital economy The programme is inclusive and welcomes a diverse range of participants, including: Employed professionals seeking career advancement Unemployed individuals looking to enter the tech sector Small and medium-sized enterprise (SME) owners Business leaders aiming to integrate digital solutions into their operations Skills and Benefits You Will Gain Participants in this programme will benefit from a wide range of professional and practical advantages, including: Industry-Relevant Skills You will develop competencies in areas that are highly sought after in today’s job market, particularly in AI and data infrastructure. Career Advancement Opportunities With globally recognized certification and hands-on experience, participants can significantly improve their career prospects. Networking Opportunities The in-person workshops and live sessions provide opportunities to connect with peers, industry experts, and trainers. Flexible Learning Format The combination of weekend webinars and limited in-person sessions makes it easier for working professionals to participate. No Cost Participation As a fully funded initiative, the programme removes financial barriers, making high-quality training accessible to a broader audience. Application Process and Deadline Interested candidates are required to submit their applications through the official application form provided by UNITAR. Important Details Application Deadline: 21 March 2026 Programme Start Date: 6 April 2026 Programme End Date: 28 June 2026 Due to limited available slots , applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible to increase their chances of selection. The Pathway to Prosperity: Upskilling Selangor’s Professionals for a Digital Future (Cohort 2) programme represents a strategic opportunity for professionals in Selangor to gain cutting-edge digital skills at no cost. With backing from UNITAR and Google, participants can expect a high-quality learning experience that is both practical and globally relevant. In a world where digital skills are no longer optional but essential, this programme offers a clear pathway toward career growth, resilience, and long-term success in the digital economy. Discover more similar opportunities here . The post Apply Now: UNITAR Google Free Digital Skills Training 2026 in Selangor (AI and Data Centres) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: World Bank AgriConnect Course 2026: Free Online Training to Boost Agricultural Jobs and Productivity
summary: Enrollment is now open for the AgriConnect course developed by the World Bank Group through its Institute for Economic Development. This online course is designed to equip professionals with practical tools and strategies to improve agricultural productivity and create more jobs across agrifood value chains. As global demand for employment continues to rise, agriculture remains one of the most important sectors with the potential to absorb labor at scale. However, unlocking this potential requires more efficient, market-driven, and climate-resilient approaches. This course offers a structured pathway for practitioners to learn how to apply evidence-based strategies to strengthen agricultural systems and drive economic growth. About the AgriConnect Course The AgriConnect course is part of a broader learning ecosystem focused on translating global research into actionable strategies for agricultural development. It is designed for individuals working in development, policymaking, and agribusiness who want to improve outcomes in the agricultural sector. Through a combination of expert-led content and interactive learning, participants will gain insights into how to design and implement effective agricultural interventions that support productivity, job creation, and income growth. What You Will Learn Participants in the course will explore key areas critical to improving agricultural systems and unlocking opportunities across value chains. The course covers: How to translate global evidence into practical agricultural development strategies Approaches to improving irrigation systems and water management Strategies to enhance market access for farmers and agribusinesses Methods for building climate-resilient agricultural systems Tools to improve productivity and efficiency across agrifood value chains The program combines video lessons, assessments, and peer discussions to ensure a comprehensive learning experience. Course Benefits The AgriConnect course offers several benefits for professionals looking to advance their skills in agricultural development. These include: Free access to high-quality learning materials A certificate of completion upon finishing the course Practical tools and frameworks for real-world application Opportunities to engage with a global network of practitioners Participants will leave the course with actionable knowledge that can be applied directly to projects, policies, and business strategies. Who Should Enroll This course is ideal for individuals working in or interested in: International development Public policy Agribusiness Agricultural consulting Nonprofit and NGO sectors It is especially valuable for professionals seeking to improve agricultural performance and create sustainable job opportunities. Course Start Date Course materials will be available starting March 19, 2026 . Participants can enroll now to gain early access and prepare for the learning experience. How to Enroll Interested participants can sign up for th e course using the official link . For more OFY training opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Australia DFAT Graduate Program 2027: How to Apply, Requirements, Salary and Benefits
summary: Applications for the 2027 Graduate Program at the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade are now officially open. This highly competitive program offers graduates the opportunity to launch a rewarding career in international affairs, policy development, corporate management, and global diplomacy. This comprehensive guide explains everything you need to know about the DFAT Graduate Program 2027, including program structure, salary, benefits, eligibility requirements, and career prospects. Overview of the DFAT Graduate Program 2027 The DFAT Graduate Program is an 18-month, full-time employment program based in Canberra, Australia. It is designed to equip graduates with practical skills, international exposure, and professional experience in government operations and foreign policy. Key Program Details Organization: Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade Program Duration: 18 months Start Date: February 2027 Location: Canberra (relocation support available) Employment Type: Full-time, ongoing APS role Salary Structure Starting Salary: APS Level 4 – AUD $84,115 + 15.4% superannuation Upon Completion: APS Level 5 – AUD $92,753 + 15.4% superannuation Program Streams Applicants can choose between two main streams: Corporate Stream Policy Stream Working at the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT) A career with DFAT provides a unique opportunity to contribute to Australia’s global engagement and international strategy. The department plays a critical role in shaping foreign policy, promoting trade, and supporting Australians abroad. DFAT operates a vast international network of over 116 embassies, consulates, and diplomatic missions across five continents. With more than 6,000 staff worldwide, the organization includes diplomats, analysts, negotiators, and development experts working collaboratively on global challenges. Key areas of DFAT’s work include: Strengthening international partnerships Expanding trade and investment opportunities Supporting global stability and security Delivering humanitarian and development programs Providing consular assistance to Australians overseas The department values diversity and actively encourages applications from candidates of varied backgrounds and experiences. About the Graduate Program Structure The DFAT Graduate Program is designed to provide hands-on experience through structured rotations and real-world assignments. Rotational Placements Participants complete four different placements over the 18-month period. These rotations allow graduates to gain exposure to various functional areas and government priorities. Corporate Stream: Roles and Career Path The Corporate Stream focuses on internal operations and organizational management within DFAT. Possible Placement Areas Consular and crisis management Overseas property management Accounting and financial management Human resources (HR) Diplomatic security Strategic communications Information and communication technology (ICT) Corporate law Australian Passport Office International operations Career Outcomes Graduates in the Corporate Stream are well-positioned to apply for overseas postings after completing the program. Potential roles include: Second Secretary Vice Consul (Corporate) Mixed-duty diplomatic positions Policy Stream: Roles and Career Path The Policy Stream is ideal for those interested in international relations, diplomacy, and global policy development. Possible Placement Areas Bilateral economic and political relations International trade negotiations Economic analysis Development and aid program management International security and cyber security International law Public diplomacy Multilateral and global issues Strategic communications Career Outcomes Policy graduates may pursue international postings in roles such as: Second Secretary (Policy, Trade, or Development) Diplomatic or mixed-duty roles overseas Benefits of the DFAT Graduate Program Participants in the program receive a comprehensive package designed to support professional growth and work-life balance. What You Will Receive Permanent employment within DFAT Competitive salary progression (APS4 to APS5) 15.4% superannuation Access to training through the Diplomatic Academy Flexible and inclusive work environment Professional networking opportunities Generous leave entitlements Relocation assistance for those moving to Canberra Who Should Apply? DFAT is looking for motivated graduates who are passionate about international engagement and public service. Key Qualities Strong teamwork and collaboration skills Relationship management ability Adaptability and resilience Analytical thinking and problem-solving skills Accountability and initiative Academic Background Requirements Corporate Stream Disciplines Suitable for candidates with backgrounds in: Finance and accounting Business and commerce Human resources Management and marketing Communications Data and ICT Engineering and architecture Policy Stream Disciplines Ideal for graduates in: International relations Law Economics Social sciences Public policy History and journalism Linguistics and communication Cyber security and STEM Development and humanitarian studies Eligibility Requirements To apply for the DFAT Graduate Program 2027, candidates must meet the following criteria: Be an Australian citizen at the time of application Have completed a bachelor’s degree between 1 January 2022 and 31 December 2026 Be eligible to obtain a Negative Vetting Level 2 security clearance Visit here , for eligibility requirement. Applicants with overseas qualifications must provide official recognition from the Australian education authorities. Applying from Overseas Candidates currently living abroad are eligible to apply. However, there are important considerations: Security clearance is mandatory for employment Interviews for clearance must be conducted in Australia Candidates must cover their own travel costs for the security interview Failure to maintain security clearance may result in termination Security Clearance Requirement All successful applicants must obtain and maintain a Negative Vetting Level 2 security clearance. This is a critical requirement for working in sensitive government roles and ensures national security standards are upheld. How to Apply for the DFAT Graduate Program 2027 Applicants must submit their application through the official DFAT careers portal under the “Current Opportunities” section. Application Deadline Closing Date: Sunday, 12 April 2026 Time: 11:30 PM AEST It is strongly recommended to apply early to avoid last-minute technical issues. Click here for details on this graduate program. All queries relating to the application and selection process for the DFAT Graduate Program should be directed to DFP Recruitment via email at dfat.graduates@dfp.com.au The DFAT Graduate Program 2027 offers a prestigious pathway into one of Australia’s most influential government institutions. With structured training, international exposure, and long-term career prospects, it is an exceptional opportunity for graduates aiming to work in diplomacy, policy, or corporate governance. Candidates who meet the eligibility requirements and are passionate about contributing to global development and international cooperation should strongly consider applying before the deadline. Discover more similar opportunities here The post Australia DFAT Graduate Program 2027: How to Apply, Requirements, Salary and Benefits appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Roche Scholarship 2026 for NHS Professionals | Fully Funded One Young World Summit
summary: The Roche Scholarship 2026 is a prestigious and fully funded opportunity designed to support emerging leaders within the United Kingdom’s National Health Service (NHS) who are working at the intersection of healthcare innovation, sustainability, and health equity. This scholarship is part of a strategic partnership between Roche and One Young World , aimed at empowering professionals who are driving meaningful change in healthcare systems. Through this initiative, selected scholars will gain access to a global leadership platform, connect with like-minded innovators, and contribute to shaping the future of sustainable healthcare delivery. About the Roche Scholarship As one of the world’s leading healthcare companies, Roche has consistently invested in the development of transformative medicines and diagnostic tools that improve patient outcomes. In the United Kingdom, Roche focuses on addressing pressing healthcare challenges by collaborating with leading scientists, healthcare professionals, and NHS institutions. A key pillar of Roche’s mission is partnership. The organization recognizes that solving complex health challenges—particularly those affecting underserved populations—requires collective expertise and cross-sector collaboration. This is why Roche actively works with NHS organizations across the UK to co-create solutions that are inclusive, effective, and scalable. In addition to advancing healthcare innovation, Roche is deeply committed to environmental sustainability. The organization acknowledges the strong connection between planetary health and human health, emphasizing that healthcare systems must evolve in ways that are both socially responsible and environmentally sustainable. Roche has set ambitious goals, including achieving net-zero carbon emissions globally by 2045 and significantly reducing the environmental impact of its operations and products. Partnership with One Young World The Roche Scholarship 2026 is delivered in collaboration with One Young World, a globally recognized organization that brings together young leaders from around the world to address critical global issues. As part of this partnership, Roche will sponsor three outstanding NHS professionals to attend the One Young World Summit 2026 , which will take place in Cape Town from 3 to 6 November 2026. The summit is an internationally acclaimed event that convenes young leaders, policymakers, business executives, and social impact pioneers to discuss and develop solutions to global challenges, including healthcare access, climate change, and social inequality. Who Should Apply? The Roche Scholarship 2026 specifically targets young leaders working within the NHS who have demonstrated a strong commitment to driving sustainable innovation and improving health equity. Eligible candidates are expected to be actively engaged in initiatives that contribute to transforming healthcare delivery. While the program is open to a wide range of professionals, ideal applicants may have experience in areas such as: Addressing Health Inequalities Candidates who are working to reduce disparities in healthcare access and outcomes—particularly for marginalized or underserved communities—are strongly encouraged to apply. This may include initiatives focused on improving access to care, addressing social determinants of health, or designing inclusive healthcare services. Advancing Innovative Healthcare Solutions Applicants who are developing or implementing new methods of diagnosis, treatment, or patient care pathways are also ideal candidates. This includes innovations that enhance efficiency, improve patient experiences, or integrate new technologies into healthcare delivery. Promoting Environmental Sustainability in Healthcare Given the increasing urgency of climate change, the scholarship also prioritizes professionals working on reducing the environmental footprint of healthcare systems. This could involve decarbonizing supply chains, reducing waste, optimizing resource use, or introducing sustainable operational practices within healthcare settings. Why This Scholarship Matters The Roche Scholarship 2026 goes beyond financial support. It represents an opportunity for NHS professionals to join a global network of changemakers, gain international exposure, and amplify the impact of their work. By participating in the One Young World Summit, scholars will not only learn from global leaders but also share their own experiences and insights. This exchange of ideas is critical in addressing complex global challenges such as health inequities and climate change. Moreover, the scholarship aligns with broader efforts to transform healthcare systems into more sustainable and inclusive models. By investing in young leaders, Roche and One Young World are helping to shape a future where healthcare innovation benefits both people and the planet. What the Scholarship Covers The Roche Scholarship 2026 is fully funded, ensuring that selected participants can focus entirely on learning, networking, and contributing at the summit without financial barriers. Successful applicants will receive the following benefits: Full Access to the One Young World Summit Scholars will participate in the One Young World Summit 2026, gaining exposure to global discussions, high-level panels, and collaborative workshops. Accommodation Hotel accommodation in Cape Town will be provided in a private room from 2 to 6 November 2026. This ensures a comfortable and secure stay throughout the duration of the program. Travel Expenses The cost of travel to and from Cape Town will be covered in economy class. Scholars are required to book their own flights, after which they will be reimbursed for the expenses. Meals and Catering All meals, including breakfast, lunch, and dinner, will be provided during the summit period, either through the hotel or at official summit venues. Local Transportation Transportation between the airport and the hotel will be covered via public transport, ensuring smooth arrival and departure logistics. Application Timeline Applicants should take note of the following important dates: Opening Date: 16 March 2026 Application Deadline: 6 May 2026 It is strongly recommended that candidates prepare their applications well in advance to ensure they can present a compelling case for selection. APPLY HERE Conclusion The Roche Scholarship 2026 is an exceptional opportunity for NHS professionals who are passionate about driving meaningful change in healthcare. With its strong focus on sustainability, innovation, and equity, the program is designed to empower individuals who are already making a difference and equip them with the tools, networks, and platforms needed to scale their impact globally. For those who meet the criteria and are committed to advancing healthcare in a responsible and inclusive way, this scholarship offers a powerful pathway to leadership and global engagement. title: Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Changemakers
summary: The Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026 is now open for applications, offering young people worldwide a unique platform to transform their ideas into impactful public policy solutions. This global initiative provides participants with the opportunity to engage with international experts, gain global recognition, and contribute to shaping policies that promote cultural diversity. With the 2026 theme focused on Preserving Cultural Diversity , this contest invites innovative youth-led solutions that address one of the most pressing global development priorities. About the Youth Innovation Contest 2026 The Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest is designed to empower young leaders to develop practical and scalable policy solutions. Participants will work individually or in teams to propose ideas that can influence global development discussions and reach key international institutions. Selected finalists will receive financial support to travel to China and present their ideas at a high-level global platform. Theme: Preserving Cultural Diversity Participants are required to develop policy proposals that address challenges and opportunities related to cultural diversity. This may include topics linked to: Cultural preservation and heritage protection Inclusion and social cohesion Indigenous knowledge systems Education and cultural awareness Sustainable development and the SDGs Digital innovation and cultural expression Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to a wide range of young people globally. Eligible applicants include: Young individuals from all countries and backgrounds Students, graduates, and early-career professionals Individuals interested in public policy, international development, and cultural issues Youth motivated to create real-world impact through policy innovation Participants may apply: Individually, or As a team of up to five members What You Need to Do To participate in the contest, applicants must complete the following steps: Select a topic related to cultural diversity Develop a detailed public policy proposal in English Complete the application form Submit both the application and full proposal before the deadline Benefits of the Youth Innovation Contest Selected participants and finalists will gain access to a wide range of benefits, including: Opportunity for your work to reach United Nations institutions Recommendation letter for a United Nations internship Invitation to pitch your idea at UNESCO during the Cultural Exchange Forum (June 10, 2026) Travel support to China for selected finalists International exposure and recognition Academic and policy-level visibility Networking opportunities with global youth leaders and experts Why You Should Apply This contest is more than just a competition—it is a pathway to global influence. Participants will not only develop policy skills but also gain exposure to international platforms that can accelerate their careers in development, governance, and global policy. Whether you are passionate about cultural preservation, social inclusion, or sustainable development, this opportunity allows you to turn your ideas into actionable solutions with global relevance. How to Apply To apply for the Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026: Click here for more info Prepare your completed application form Develop your full policy proposal in English Ensure you meet all requirements outlined in the official guidelines Submit your complete application via email Application Email: contact@gydca.org Application Deadline Deadline: April 15, 2026 Late or incomplete applications may not be considered, so early submission is strongly encouraged. Full details here https://qr61.cn/oeWlMx/qgQuERK?lid=05so8knvt4zftc4j1h rlid=05so8knvt4zftc4j1h The Global Development Public Policy Youth Innovation Contest 2026 is a powerful opportunity for young changemakers to influence global conversations on cultural diversity. By participating, you position yourself at the intersection of policy, innovation, and global development. If you have an idea that can shape the future, this is your chance to share it with the world. title: World Bank Group (WBG) Youth Summit 2026 Speaker Applications Now Open: Shape the Future of Jobs in the Digital Age
summary: The World Bank Group (WBG) Youth Summit 2026 is now inviting applications for speakers to join one of the most influential global platforms for youth-led development dialogue. If you are a young leader, practitioner, entrepreneur, or researcher working on employment, innovation, or inclusive development, this is your opportunity to contribute to shaping global conversations. About the WBG Youth Summit The WBG Youth Summit is the World Bank’s largest annual event dedicated to young people aged 18–35. It brings together emerging leaders, experts, and changemakers from around the world to explore practical solutions to global development challenges. The Summit features: High-level plenary sessions with global experts Interactive workshops and hands-on labs A pitch competition for innovative ideas Networking opportunities with World Bank staff and partners Theme for 2026: FutureWorks – Designing Jobs for the Digital Age The 2026 Summit focuses on a critical global challenge: creating sustainable and inclusive employment opportunities for the 1.2 billion young people expected to enter the workforce over the next decade. The agenda will explore how innovation, policy, and investment can come together to generate quality jobs in a rapidly changing world. Key Focus Areas The Summit will center on the following thematic areas: Education and Skills Development Entrepreneurship and Job Creation Agriculture and Agribusiness Technology and Innovation Development Financing Fragility, Conflict, and Violence Speakers are expected to provide practical, action-oriented insights that demonstrate how these sectors can contribute to inclusive and sustainable employment for youth. Call for Speakers: Who Should Apply The Youth Summit Committee is seeking individuals who can contribute meaningful perspectives and real-world solutions. Ideal candidates include: Young professionals and emerging leaders Entrepreneurs and startup founders Researchers and academics Development practitioners and policy experts Innovators working in digital, agricultural, or financial sectors Applicants should have demonstrated experience or ideas related to job creation, digital transformation, or youth economic empowerment. Why You Should Apply Selected speakers will have the opportunity to: Share their work on a global stage Influence conversations on youth employment and development Connect with international organizations, policymakers, and innovators Contribute to shaping solutions for the future of work How to Apply Interested applicants can submit their speaker nominations through the official application form: Apply here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScuLkpCPPg6UZxDq0kqaW5gQL9Rgs_zeTQzdAkKpVUlCACwzg/viewform Applicants are encouraged to clearly articulate their expertise, experience, and the unique insights they can bring to the Summit. Application Deadline Deadline to apply: 1 May 2026 About the World Bank Group Youth Summit The Youth Summit is a flagship initiative of the World Bank Group aimed at amplifying youth voices in global development. It serves as a platform for collaboration, innovation, and knowledge exchange, helping to bridge the gap between young leaders and global decision-makers. Learn more: https://www.worldbank.org/youthsummit Apply here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScuLkpCPPg6UZxDq0kqaW5gQL9Rgs_zeTQzdAkKpVUlCACwzg/viewform If you are working on solutions that address employment, digital transformation, or inclusive growth, this is a unique opportunity to contribute to a global dialogue that drives real impact. Apply now and be part of shaping the future of work for the next generation. The post World Bank Group (WBG) Youth Summit 2026 Speaker Applications Now Open: Shape the Future of Jobs in the Digital Age appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Reham Al-Farra Memorial Journalism Fellowship 2026 (RAF) – Fully Funded UN Programme
summary: Applications are now open for the Reham Al-Farra (RAF) Memorial Journalism Fellowship 2026 , a prestigious United Nations programme designed for young professional journalists from developing countries and economies in transition. The application period runs until 3 April 2026 . Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official FAQ before submitting their application. About the RAF Journalism Fellowship The Reham Al-Farra Memorial Journalism Fellowship was established by the United Nations General Assembly in December 1980 under Resolution 35/201. The programme was originally known as the DPI Training Programme for Broadcasters and Journalists from Developing Countries. In 2003, the fellowship was renamed in memory of Reham Al-Farra, a 29-year-old United Nations Public Information Officer who was killed during the bombing of the UN headquarters in Baghdad on 19 August 2003. Since its launch in 1981, the fellowship has supported nearly 700 journalists from 168 countries, contributing to improved global understanding of the United Nations and its work. Programme Overview The RAF Fellowship is a highly competitive opportunity that enables selected journalists to gain first-hand experience covering the United Nations. During the programme, fellows will: Cover the United Nations General Assembly Attend high-level UN briefings and sessions Interview senior UN officials Engage with UN agencies, funds, and programmes Meet Permanent Representatives and diplomats Participate in training sessions with media experts Connect with journalists and media organizations in New York The programme provides a unique platform to strengthen professional skills, expand networks, and enhance global reporting capacity. Programme Duration The 2026 RAF Fellowship is expected to take place: Over a four-week period Between September and October 2026 In a format that may be virtual or hybrid (to be confirmed) Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be between 22 and 35 years old Be a full-time working journalist Have at least two years of professional journalism experience Be a national of an eligible developing country or economy in transition Demonstrate experience covering UN-related issues or global topics Additional considerations: Freelance journalists are eligible, provided they submit strong work samples and proof of professional accreditation Applicants who are studying or teaching journalism but are not working full-time as journalists are not eligible Applicants may live outside their country of citizenship, provided they are working as journalists covering UN-related issues relevant to their home country. Eligible Countries The fellowship is open to journalists from a wide range of developing countries and countries with economies in transition across: Africa Asia Latin America and the Caribbean Middle East Eastern Europe and Central Asia The full list includes countries such as Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, South Africa, India, Indonesia, Brazil, Mexico, Egypt, Philippines, and many others. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following: Completed online application form Curriculum Vitae (CV) in English At least five published work samples related to the United Nations or its priority issues (from the last six months) Copy of passport or national identification Two reference letters All documents must be uploaded to a file-sharing platform such as Google Drive or Dropbox. Applicants must provide a link with permissions that allow reviewers to access and download all files. Failure to submit all required materials may result in disqualification. Submission Guidelines for Broadcast Journalists Television and radio journalists should: Provide links to publicly accessible content where possible Upload media files to a cloud platform if links are unavailable Ensure all files are accessible through shared links Language Requirements English proficiency is required, as it is the primary language of communication during the programme Fellows may publish their work in their native languages Reference letters do not need to be translated into English but must include complete contact details for verification. Selection Criteria Applications will be assessed based on: Professional experience Quality and relevance of work samples Coverage of UN-related issues Audience reach and impact Geographic diversity Gender balance How to Apply Applications must be submitted through the official online portal: https://www.un.org/en/raf/application2026 Applicants are required to type their name in the declaration field to sign the application and ensure submission is completed successfully. Application Deadline Applications open: 16 February 2026 Application deadline: 3 April 2026 Why You Should Apply The RAF Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to: Gain direct exposure to international diplomacy and policymaking Build relationships with global media professionals Access high-level UN officials and decision-makers Strengthen reporting skills on global issues Contribute to improved public understanding of the United Nations Apply here: https://www.un.org/en/raf/application2026 For more info visit : https://www.un.org/en/raf?utm_content=buffer792af utm_medium=social utm_source=twitter.com utm_campaign=buffer Explore More Opportunities For more fellowships and journalism opportunities, visit: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=fellowship The post Reham Al-Farra Memorial Journalism Fellowship 2026 (RAF) – Fully Funded UN Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
